description_long: This object is a vintage cathode ray tube (CRT) television with a rectangular wooden cabinet and a prominent convex glass screen. The front features a large, slightly curved display bordered by a thick black bezel, with a control panel on the right side that includes a rotary channel dial, several push buttons, and a speaker grille. The top, sides, and front panel are finished in a medium-brown wood veneer, giving it a classic mid-20th-century appearance. The rear of the television is made of black plastic or metal, with ventilation slots and a recessed handle for carrying. The overall design is boxy and robust, typical of televisions from the 1970s or 1980s. This television would likely be found discarded on a curb, in a garage, or as a nostalgic prop in an urban environment.
description: A vintage CRT television with a wood veneer cabinet, convex glass screen, and a side control panel with rotary dial and buttons.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, thick black bezel, control panel with rotary dial, buttons, and speaker grille.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the wood veneer panel and a rectangular ventilation slot.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the black plastic or metal back with ventilation slots, a recessed handle, and a label area.
description_view_3: Right side view showing a plain wood veneer panel with no visible features.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick black plastic bezel with a matte finish, rectangular shape, and no visible branding.
attribute_control_button_layout: Clustered on the front right panel, including a rotary dial and several push buttons.
